sections.paragraph:
Original: גּתּית

Transliteration: gittı̂yth

Phonetic: ghit-teeth'

BDB Definition:

Gittith = "a wine-press"
  1. a musical instrument? from Gath? and used in three Psalm titles - Psa 8:1-9, 81, Psa 84:1-12; a song title used for the Feast of Booths

Origin: from H1663

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Noun Feminine
